mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to manage Argo CD clusters efficiently. It simplifies the deployment and management of Argo CD instances, making it ideal for teams adopting GitOps practices. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes operators and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Argo CD Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Argo CD Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add argocd-operator https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Argo CD Operator in the 'argocd' namespace using the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace argocd

helm install argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --version 0.17.0

```

> Create a namespace for the Argo CD Operator.
> Install the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create the CatalogSource for the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a CatalogSource resource to define where the operator can find its images.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

```

> This CatalogSource points to the Argo CD Operator registry.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: CatalogSource
metadata:
  name: argocd-catalog
spec:
  sourceType: grpc
  image: quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92
  displayName: Argo CD Operators
  publisher: Argo CD Community
EOF

steps.title: Create the OperatorGroup
steps.description:
This step creates an OperatorGroup resource to define the scope of the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd
EOF

```

> This OperatorGroup allows the operator to manage resources in the specified namespace.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
kind: OperatorGroup
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
  namespace: argocd
EOF

steps.title: Create the Subscription for the Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
This step creates a Subscription resource to manage the operator's lifecycle.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

```

> This Subscription ensures the operator is installed and updated.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: Subscription
metadata:
  name: argocd-operator
spec:
  channel: alpha
  name: argocd-operator
  source: argocd-catalog
  sourceNamespace: olm
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks if the Argo CD Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Argo CD Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall argocd-operator --namespace argocd

kubectl delete namespace argocd

```

> This command uninstalls the operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This process will upgrade the operator to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -r kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade argocd-operator argocd-operator/argocd-operator --namespace argocd --version 0.17.0

kubectl get pods -n argocd

```

> Drain the operator pod before upgrading.
> Upgrade the operator to the new version.
> Verify that the upgraded operator pod is running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n argocd -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -r kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "CatalogSource" in version "operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift Operator Lifecycle Manager (OLM) may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure OLM is installed and running in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Argo CD Operator may not be installed correctly or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the installation logs and ensure the operator is installed in the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The operator may be misconfigured or missing required permissions.

**Fix:**
Review the operator logs for errors and ensure all required RBAC permissions are granted.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Subscription or CatalogSource may not be created properly.

**Fix:**
Recreate the Subscription and CatalogSource resources and check for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management and improved error handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Argo CD Operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n argocd'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
